title: Senior Product Manager, Claude
score_rationale: Strong AI/ML domain alignment — your search platform experience maps directly to building LLM-powered products. Samsung and Amazon search roles show exactly the data-driven PM profile Anthropic looks for. Minor gap: no direct safety/alignment background, but quantified ML impact covers 80% of requirements.
recommendation_reason: Top-tier AI company with direct domain match; your search + ML PM background is a strong fit for Claude product work.
suggested_summary: Senior PM with 7+ years leading AI/ML-powered search and discovery products at scale (10M+ MAU, $2B+ GMV impact). Track record shipping data-driven features that measurably improve user outcomes through ML and experimentation.
top_bullets: Led AI-powered search platform serving 10M+ monthly users, improving CTR by 23%
$2B+ GMV search relevance at Amazon, shipped with cross-functional ML teams
Built real-time personalization engine increasing conversion by 18%
gap_handling: Acknowledge no direct safety research background; emphasize genuine interest in responsible AI and frame LLM product work as adjacent context.

title: Product Manager, Guest Discovery and Homepage
location: San Francisco CA, Los Angeles CA, New York NY
score_rationale: Riley's core competencies — AI-powered discovery, search relevance, personalization, and recommendation systems — map almost perfectly to Airbnb's homepage and guest discovery mandate. The Samsung.com role is a near-direct analogue: owning search and discovery for a large-scale consumer platform with 10M+ monthly users, shipping hybrid AI search, ranking optimization, and conversational AI. The Kolon e-commerce experience adds consumer growth and conversion funnel depth. The one gap is consumer marketplace/travel domain and the lifecycle/growth marketing collaboration emphasis, which Riley hasn't led directly — but the overall profile is compelling and competitive.
recommendation_reason: Riley's AI discovery, search relevance, and personalization experience at scale is a near-perfect archetype match for Airbnb's homepage product; 9 years clears the 8+ requirement with strong metric-backed evidence.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9 years of experience owning search, discovery, and personalization products at scale — including launching hybrid AI search and recommendation systems for 10M+ monthly users across international consumer platforms. Proven track record of driving measurable guest and business outcomes through AI-powered relevance models, A/B experimentation, and cross-functional alignment with engineering, design, data science, and marketing teams.
top_bullets: Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation CSAT (NPS) by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion.

Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing on a $2B+ GMV platform.
gap_handling: The role emphasizes lifecycle/growth marketing collaboration and external product narrative/media communication — areas less prominent in Riley's resume. Address this by highlighting the AEO pipeline (external AI agent traffic 8x) and conversational AI launch as examples of cross-channel discovery strategy, and call out any marketing team partnerships explicitly in the Samsung bullet context.

title: Senior Product Manager, AI (Customer Agent)
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background — conversational AI, LLM pipelines, search ranking, and personalization — maps closely to Klaviyo's Customer Agent role, which is deeply rooted in LLM/RAG agent systems and cross-channel experiences. The Samsung conversational AI chat experience and AEO pipeline are directly relevant evidence. However, Riley's experience is predominantly e-commerce/retail-facing (B2C search, seller tools), while this role sits squarely in B2B SaaS tooling (brands configuring and deploying AI agents), and Riley lacks explicit signals of experience building agent configuration systems, onboarding activation funnels, or B2B SaaS growth loops — all of which are core to this job description.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and conversational product experience transfers well, but the B2B SaaS configuration/onboarding focus and lack of RAG/agent architecture depth create a meaningful gap requiring deliberate tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered and conversational AI experiences — including an end-to-end on-site AI chat agent, hybrid search ranking, and automated data pipelines — that drive measurable engagement and revenue outcomes at scale. Proven track record partnering with AI/ML and data science teams to define agent behavior, build feedback loops, and deliver intuitive B2C and B2B product experiences to millions of users.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users.

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks.

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%.
gap_handling: The role's core asks — brand-facing agent configuration systems, onboarding activation funnels, and RAG/agent architecture depth — are not explicitly evidenced on the resume. Riley should reframe the Samsung conversational AI launch to highlight agent configuration decisions, persona/knowledge setup, and feedback loops for continuous improvement. Mentioning familiarity with RAG concepts and multi-agent workflows (already listed as "Familiar" skills) in a cover letter or screening call, with honest framing, can bridge this gap without overstating.

title: Staff Product Manager, Search Algorithms
location: New York City, NY; San Francisco, CA
score_rationale: Riley's background in AI-powered search, ranking optimization, and ML-driven discovery systems aligns tightly with Faire's core search algorithms role — the precision@3 and CTR improvements at Samsung.com are directly relevant evidence. However, this role is titled Staff PM and explicitly asks for 7+ years on "complex Search systems" in a high-growth startup context; Riley's search depth is strong but concentrated in ~2 years at Samsung, and her B2B wholesale/marketplace domain experience is thinner. The stretch to Staff-level at a Series D marketplace startup is real and competitive.
recommendation_reason: Excellent search/AI skill match but role is a Staff-level stretch and requires deep startup-native experience Riley partially lacks; tailoring is essential.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping ML-powered search and discovery systems at scale — including hybrid search ranking, query understanding, and behavioral signal optimization serving 10M+ monthly users at Samsung.com. Passionate about leveling the playing field for independent businesses through intelligent discovery experiences, with a track record of measurable impact: precision@3 +30%, 1st-result CTR +45%, and AOV lifts on a $2B+ GMV platform.
top_bullets: Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Defined success metrics for search engagement and led instrumentation strategy in partnership with engineering and analytics teams — increasing search success rate by 15% and CTR by 25% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users
gap_handling: The Staff PM title and 7+ years on complex Search systems bar is the key gap. Riley has ~2 years of deep search PM work (Samsung) plus strong adjacent ML/personalization experience. In the application and interviews, she should frame her total 9-year arc as progressively ML-intensive, emphasize the architectural and metric-definition depth of her Samsung search work (offline + online metrics, ranking pipelines, instrumentation), and draw parallels between Faire's B2B retailer discovery problem and her Amazon seller-catalog + Samsung e-commerce search experience to close the perceived domain gap.

title: Product Manager, API Growth
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background, A/B testing chops, and e-commerce growth experience are a strong foundation, and the AI domain alignment with Anthropic is excellent. However, the role is explicitly focused on developer platform and API growth — onboarding, console, billing, self-serve upgrade paths for developers — which is a meaningfully different archetype from Riley's consumer search and e-commerce discovery work. The Amazon B2B listing tool role is the closest proxy but it was B2B seller tooling rather than pure developer platform growth. Riley meets the 6+ year experience bar and has strong analytical/growth credentials, but lacks demonstrated developer-platform-native experience (API docs, SDKs, developer funnels, usage-based billing).
recommendation_reason: Strong AI domain and growth credentials, but developer/API platform experience is the key gap; worth applying with targeted framing of Amazon B2B and API-adjacent work.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving growth, search, and intelligent product experiences at Samsung, Amazon, and high-scale e-commerce platforms. Proven track record of owning full-funnel growth metrics — acquisition, activation, and monetization — through rigorous A/B testing, data-driven iteration, and cross-functional execution on AI/ML-powered products used by millions of users and millions of B2B sellers.
top_bullets: Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — demonstrating API/platform-side growth impact and developer-facing product delivery at scale.

Defined on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, leading phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels, increasing NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.

Saved $1.4M in operational cost by enhancing self-serve workflow for B2B sellers, reducing manual support contacts — a direct analog to self-serve upgrade paths and activation optimization central to this role.
gap_handling: The developer/API platform gap is real — Riley has never owned onboarding flows, API docs, console UX, or usage-based billing for a technical developer audience. To bridge this, the cover letter and resume should explicitly reframe the Amazon role as a "developer platform" analog (B2B sellers as technical users, self-serve tooling, ML pipeline delivery) and call out any personal use of Claude API or LLM tooling. Consider adding a brief note about hands-on familiarity with API products in the summary to signal credibility with developer audiences.

title: Principal, Product Manager - AI / LLM
score_rationale: Riley's AI/LLM product background — hybrid search, conversational AI, RAG-adjacent pipelines, and LLM-driven systems — aligns strongly with the technical core of this role. The AEO pipeline (structured metadata extraction), on-site conversational AI chat, and search ranking work all directly speak to Twilio's Copilot backend vision. However, this role is titled "Principal PM" and leans heavily into backend platform infrastructure (LLM orchestration engines, RAG pipelines, API telemetry, concurrency/latency management, developer-persona tooling) — an archetype that is slightly outside Riley's consumer-facing AI search and e-commerce discovery focus. The seniority is also a stretch: Principal PM sits above Riley's target band of Senior/PM III, and Twilio requires 8+ years with backend/platform depth that Riley partially but not fully demonstrates.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM signal and conversational AI experience are compelling, but Principal-level seniority and backend platform/developer-tooling focus are meaningful stretches from Riley's consumer discovery archetype.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving AI-powered experiences — from LLM-driven pipelines and conversational AI to hybrid search and recommendation systems — that serve millions of users at scale. Deep expertise in defining AI agent architectures, knowledge retrieval strategies, and contextual personalization that autonomously reduce friction and drive measurable business outcomes.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata (product attributes, inventory, pricing) from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%; partnered with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines that translated seller inputs into structured catalog templates across 20+ high-impact categories
gap_handling: The biggest gap is backend platform/infrastructure depth — Twilio explicitly wants experience managing LLM concurrency, latency, RAG pipelines, and developer-persona tooling (think GitHub Copilot / AWS Q). Riley should frame the AEO metadata pipeline and conversational AI chat backend work as the closest analog, and consider briefly noting familiarity with RAG concepts and prompt engineering in a skills section or cover letter. Acknowledging the Principal-level scope in a cover letter by positioning it as a deliberate upward move (not a lateral one) will help manage expectations.

title: Staff Product Manager, AI Features
score_rationale: Good AI domain alignment and remote flexibility. Figma's AI features team is building generative design tools — your ML product experience is relevant, but design tool domain is new territory. Staff-level role is a stretch given target Senior PM level.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI PM match, but design domain is unfamiliar and Staff level is a stretch — worth applying if you can connect to design workflow context.
suggested_summary: Senior PM with deep AI/ML product delivery experience and a track record of leading cross-functional teams to ship data-driven features. Excited to bring search + personalization expertise to AI-powered creative tooling.
top_bullets: Led AI-powered search platform serving 10M+ monthly users, improving CTR by 23%
Partnered with data science teams to build real-time recommendation systems
Drove A/B experimentation framework reducing feature release cycle by 40%
gap_handling: Design tool domain gap is real — acknowledge it, but pivot to how ML/search PM skills (user behavior signals, relevance, recommendations) transfer directly to AI feature work in any domain.

However, the role is explicitly framed as Staff PM — one level above Riley's target — and skews heavily toward internal platform/tooling and agentic AI systems rather than Riley's consumer-facing search and discovery specialty. The emphasis on multi-agent orchestration, context engineering, vendor/SaaS contract management, and presenting to CXO audiences are gaps not directly evidenced in the resume.

suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping production-grade AI/ML systems — including hybrid search, LLM-driven pipelines, and recommendation platforms — at Samsung and Amazon, serving 10M+ users and 2M+ B2B sellers. top_bullets: Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion
Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines across 20+ categories
gap_handling: Riley has no explicit resume evidence of agentic system design, multi-agent orchestration, or internal AI platform evangelism across an enterprise. Address this by: (1) reframing the AEO pipeline and conversational AI work as "agentic workflow" experience, (2) highlighting cross-functional adoption efforts (e.g., Samsung search rollout to 3 markets, Amazon seller tool scaling) as internal platform adoption analogues, and (3) being prepared to speak concretely to LLM evaluation frameworks and agent tooling in interviews. Vendor/SaaS contract experience is also a stated requirement with zero resume coverage — acknowledge this gap proactively if asked.

top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI, increasing web navigation CSAT score (NPS) by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users

Restructured new customer benefit package and revamped onboarding workflow, increasing first purchase rate by 30%; designed segmentation-driven CRM marketing program and boosted 1-year retention by 7% and re-purchase rate by 15%
gap_handling: Riley has no explicit Help Center, support deflection, or CustOps tooling experience — the operational core of this role. To address this in application materials, frame the Samsung conversational AI chat launch as a self-service deflection tool, and emphasize the $1.4M cost savings at Amazon as evidence of reducing support burden through product. Be prepared to speak directly to agent-facing tooling strategy in interviews, even if drawing on analogous cross-functional platform work.

title: Product Manager, Claude Code
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product credentials are genuinely strong — hybrid search, LLM pipelines, conversational AI, and AEO all signal serious AI-product depth that aligns with Anthropic's mission. However, this role is tightly scoped around developer tools (CLI, agentic coding, dev ecosystems), and Riley's background is consumer/e-commerce with no visible dev-tools or B2D (build-for-developers) experience. The hard requirement of 1+ year as a professional engineer is also a meaningful gap — Riley's resume shows analytical and technical PM skills but no engineering tenure. The seniority fit is slightly off too: the $285K–$305K comp band and scope suggest a Staff/Lead-level role, which is one notch above Riley's target.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML product DNA is a real asset at Anthropic, but the dev-tools domain gap and missing engineering background are genuine disqualifiers that make this a competitive long-shot worth a tailored shot if Riley can credibly speak to developer empathy.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping intelligent, LLM-driven product experiences at scale — including conversational AI, hybrid search pipelines, and structured data automation that has driven measurable business outcomes for 10M+ users. Combining deep cross-functional collaboration with engineers and AI researchers, a hands-on approach to model evaluation, and a track record of translating cutting-edge AI capabilities into polished, high-distribution products.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience — from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch — in partnership with UX, engineering, and marketing teams to support product discovery and comparison.

Defined and delivered an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks.

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria — improving precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%.
gap_handling: The biggest gap is the 1+ year professional engineering requirement and the dev-tools domain. In the application and interviews, Riley should: (1) lean hard into technical depth — SQL, BigQuery, API design, LLM evaluation, and prompt engineering work — to demonstrate engineering-adjacent credibility; (2) highlight any personal use of Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, or similar tools to show genuine developer empathy; (3) reframe the AEO pipeline and conversational AI work as developer-facing infrastructure where possible. If Riley has ever written scripts, built internal tools, or contributed to API specs, those belong on the resume for this role.

title: Product Manager, Consumer
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product chops — hybrid search, conversational AI, LLM pipelines, recommendation systems — align well with Anthropic's Consumer team and the domain signals strongly match. However, the role's most prominent requirement is a "former consumer founder" or deep 0-to-1 consumer product creation track record with viral distribution; Riley's background is predominantly within large enterprises (Samsung, Amazon) shipping incremental improvements to existing surfaces rather than founding or launching net-new consumer products from scratch. The "prototype with Claude Code" and "hands-on with emerging research" requirements also skew toward a builder/engineer-adjacent PM that Riley's resume doesn't directly evidence.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI domain and seniority alignment, but the founder/0-to-1 consumer launch requirement is a material gap that will make this competitive without explicit reframing.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years building consumer-facing AI experiences — from hybrid search and conversational AI at Samsung.com (10M+ monthly users) to ML-driven personalization and live commerce on a $2B GMV platform. Known for translating frontier AI capabilities into shipped products that measurably improve discovery, engagement, and conversion at scale.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users
Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI product credentials and solid A/B testing / analytics chops, but this role is squarely a Growth/Monetization PM role — pricing, packaging, free-to-paid conversion, and subscription funnel optimization — which is not Riley's primary archetype. While Kolon and Samsung show some growth-adjacent work (onboarding, retention, AOV), Riley has never explicitly owned a monetization funnel, pricing strategy, or subscription upgrade flow at scale. The domain (Consumer AI / SaaS) partially aligns, but the growth-specialist expectation and "majority in growth-focused roles" requirement is a meaningful gap.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/search brand and analytical evidence, but monetization/growth PM specialization is a clear gap that will be tested hard in interviews.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving measurable revenue impact through data-driven experimentation, personalization systems, and conversion funnel optimization across e-commerce and AI-powered products. Proven track record of shipping growth initiatives at scale — from onboarding redesigns that lifted first-purchase rates 30% to recommendation engines that grew AOV 25% on a $2B+ GMV platform — with a deep passion for building safe, beneficial AI experiences.
top_bullets: Restructured new customer benefit package and revamped onboarding workflow, increasing first purchase rate by 30%; designed segmentation-driven CRM marketing program and boosted 1-year retention by 7% and re-purchase rate by 15%
Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, leading a phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels, increasing web navigation CSAT (NPS) by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users
gap_handling: The "majority in growth-focused roles" requirement is the single biggest gap. title: Senior Product Manager - Data Agent
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — hybrid search, LLM pipelines, ranking systems, and conversational AI — that partially map to this role's AI-powered analytics vision. However, this role is deeply rooted in B2B SaaS, business intelligence, semantic/context layers, data warehouses, and the modern data stack (dbt, Looker, Snowflake territory), which Riley's resume does not directly demonstrate. The 0-to-1 B2B platform ownership and BI/observability domain expertise represent meaningful gaps. SQL fluency and AI/LLM experience are genuine strengths that land well here.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and analytics foundations, but missing core BI/data stack domain depth and pure B2B SaaS 0-to-1 experience that this role centers on.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving LLM-powered product experiences, search intelligence, and data pipelines at Samsung and Amazon, delivering measurable outcomes for 10M+ users at scale. Proven ability to define AI eval frameworks, govern data quality pipelines, and partner with data scientists and engineers to ship trustworthy, analytics-rich products — now targeting AI-native B2B data products.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines

Led cross-functional team to design a unified customer data platform (CDP), integrating offline (POS, SAP CRM) and online behavioral data to create a single customer view in BigQuery, defining requirements for analytics tool integration (Google Analytics, Looker, Tableau)
gap_handling: The biggest gap is BI/semantic layer domain expertise (Looker, dbt, Snowflake, warehouse-native analytics). Riley should briefly address this in a cover letter by connecting her data pipeline and structured metadata work (AEO pipeline, CDP build, BigQuery integration) to the semantic/context layer concept, and signal genuine curiosity about the modern data stack. Avoid overselling — lean into the AI eval and governance angle where her LLM pipeline and search relevance instrumentation experience is genuinely relevant.

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
location: Bellevue, Washington; Chicago, Illinois; New York, New York; Washington, DC
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — LLM pipelines, conversational AI, agentic workflows (AEO), and multi-modal ML — that partially satisfy the "hands-on AI/ML" requirement. However, the core of this role is Identity & Access Management (IAM), specifically OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, and agent-to-agent authentication protocols, which are completely absent from Riley's background. The role is also enterprise/developer-platform-facing (SDKs, APIs, security architects), whereas Riley's archetype is consumer-facing AI search and e-commerce discovery. The AI/ML overlap is meaningful but the IAM/security gap is a hard blocker for full alignment.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product credentials are a genuine asset, but the total absence of IAM/security domain experience (OAuth2, OIDC, Zero Trust) is a significant gap that hiring managers will notice immediately.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered pipelines, conversational AI, and agentic systems at scale — including AEO automation that drove 8x AI agent traffic and a conversational discovery experience for 10M+ monthly users. Bringing deep cross-functional execution experience with AI/ML teams to help Okta define the identity and governance layer for the next generation of autonomous AI agents.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks
Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch, supporting product discovery for 10M+ monthly users
Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system with multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines, reducing manual listing time by 80%
gap_handling: Be upfront in your cover letter: acknowledge you're coming from the AI product side rather than IAM, but frame it as a strength — you understand how AI agents actually behave in the wild (AEO pipelines, conversational flows, agentic traffic patterns) and can bridge the AI and identity/security teams in a way a traditional IAM PM cannot. Spend 1–2 hours reading Okta's developer docs on OAuth2/OIDC and reference specific token-exchange concepts in your application to signal ramp-up speed.

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — LLM pipelines, conversational AI, agentic workflows (AEO), and search ranking — that partially satisfy the "hands-on AI/ML" requirement, and her 9 years of experience clears the 5+ year bar. However, this role is fundamentally an enterprise IAM/Security platform PM role: deep knowledge of OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust, and developer-centric identity protocols is the core requirement, and Riley has no visible background in identity, access management, or security infrastructure. The domain pivot from consumer AI search/e-commerce to enterprise security identity is the central gap that limits this score to "maybe with serious tailoring."
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product credentials are a genuine asset, but the absence of IAM, OAuth/OIDC, and enterprise security experience is a significant gap for a role where identity protocol expertise is a stated hard requirement.
suggested_summary: Product Manager with 9+ years building AI-powered products at scale, including LLM-driven pipelines, multi-agent workflows, and conversational AI experiences deployed to 10M+ users. Eager to apply deep AI product expertise to the emerging challenge of AI agent identity, authentication, and governance at Okta's "Okta for AI Agents" initiative.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks
Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering
Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system with multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines, reducing manual listing time by 80%
gap_handling: Riley should proactively acknowledge the IAM gap in a cover letter while framing her AEO pipeline and multi-agent/LLM product work as directly adjacent to AI agent identity challenges. She should self-study OAuth2/OIDC flows and Zero Trust basics (Okta's own developer docs are a great resource) so she can speak credibly to the protocols in a screen call. Positioning herself as "the PM who deeply understands how AI agents behave and what they need" — while partnering with an IAM-expert engineering team — is a credible narrative that doesn't oversell.

title: Senior Product Manager, Project & Task Experience
score_rationale: Riley's core strengths — AI/ML product delivery, data-driven experimentation, and cross-functional leadership — are genuine and well-evidenced, and Asana's AI curiosity signal is a light fit. However, the role centers on B2B enterprise work management (project/task UX, complex data models, compliance), which is meaningfully different from Riley's e-commerce search and discovery domain. Riley has B2B exposure at Amazon but in a seller tooling context, not productivity/collaboration software; there is no signal of experience with enterprise compliance, task/project management systems, or productivity platform UX — the core competencies of this team.
recommendation_reason: B2B platform and cross-functional PM skills transfer, but the enterprise work-management domain and project/task UX depth are a real gap that will make Riley a non-obvious fit vs. candidates with SaaS productivity tool backgrounds.
suggested_summary: Senior Product Manager with 9+ years driving AI-powered product experiences at Samsung and Amazon, with a strong track record of shipping complex, data-model-heavy features — from ML ranking systems to conversational AI — that serve millions of users. Proven B2B platform PM at Amazon (2M+ sellers), with deep cross-functional collaboration chops across engineering, design, data science, and enterprise stakeholders.
top_bullets: Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%; partnered with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines across 20+ high-impact categories.

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI, increasing web navigation CSAT/NPS by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users.

Saved $1.4M in operational cost by enhancing a self-serve workflow based on 10+ interviews and 300+ surveys, partnering with 5 dependent teams to resolve root-cause listing experience issues for B2B sellers.
gap_handling: Riley lacks direct experience in enterprise work management, project/task UX, or productivity SaaS — the heart of this role. To address this in the application: (1) In the cover letter, draw an explicit analogy between managing structured catalog/data models at Amazon and the complex data relationships in Asana's project/task system. (2) Reframe the Amazon B2B Platform experience around enterprise-grade product thinking, compliance, and multi-stakeholder complexity. (3) Demonstrate genuine curiosity about Asana's product by referencing specific pain points in work management that AI/ML could solve — this plays directly to their "AI curiosity" signal.

title: Senior Product Manager, APM - Observability
score_rationale: Riley's core strengths are in AI search, e-commerce discovery, and personalization — domains that are largely orthogonal to APM (Application Performance Monitoring), distributed tracing, Synthetics, and RUM, which are the explicit focus of this role. While Riley has worked with ML pipelines, behavioral signals, and structured data, there is no evidence of experience in observability tooling, APM platforms, distributed tracing (e.g., OpenTelemetry, Jaeger), or developer-facing infrastructure products. The PM archetype here is B2B/DevTools/Platform, which is a partial stretch from Riley's consumer/e-commerce/AI-search background — the Amazon B2B experience is the closest proxy but still does not touch observability. Seniority is a reasonable match (Senior PM), and Elastic is a strong brand in the search/AI space, but the domain gap is the decisive barrier.
recommendation_reason: Core domain (APM, distributed tracing, Synthetics, RUM) has no overlap with Riley's search/e-commerce/AI background — this is a fundamental mismatch, not a tailoring problem.
suggested_summary: Product manager with 9+ years driving AI-powered platform experiences, including ML-driven pipelines, behavioral signal instrumentation, and structured data systems at Samsung and Amazon. Experienced in cross-functional delivery and data-driven product iteration at scale — though transitioning domain expertise toward observability and APM would be required to be competitive for this role.
top_bullets: Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — closest signal to platform/developer tooling PM work
Defined success metrics for search engagement and led instrumentation strategy in partnership with engineering and analytics teams — partial relevance to observability thinking
Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup — demonstrates structured data pipeline ownership
gap_handling: The APM/observability domain gap is not easily bridged through resume tailoring alone. If Riley is genuinely interested in Elastic, a stronger angle would be targeting Elastic's Search AI or E-commerce/Retail Search PM roles, where her hybrid search, ranking, and AI discovery experience is directly competitive. To pursue observability roles in general, Riley would need to build demonstrated exposure — e.g., through side projects, certifications (OpenTelemetry), or a role in a related DevTools/platform team first.
